name: Dean Connors
headline: Undrafted Ram battling for a backfield roster spot
body:
Connors arrives in Los Angeles as an undrafted rookie staring up at a crowded depth chart, currently slotted as an RB4 who must beat out Ronnie Rivers just to claim a roster spot. The Rams' backfield is stacked, so any path to meaningful snaps is narrow. He carries no ADP, landing around RB123 in dynasty circles and RB185 in redraft, which fairly reflects a fringe asset with no projectable 2026 workload yet.

The profile is more interesting than the role. He grades as a well-rounded back who finishes runs and excels in pass protection, and a 14.4% college target share hints at real third-down receiving value. Average burst and long speed cap his ceiling, but the receiving chops give him a survivable niche. Realistically he's a watch-list stash whose value spikes only with an injury ahead of him or a fresh landing spot elsewhere.
